message:
refactor: propagate asupersync Cx through RPC, agent, interactive, and session layers (bd-xdcrh.2.1)

Replace per-spawn AgentCx::for_request() with inherited Cx propagation to
ensure cancel-correctness, deadline propagation, and runtime ownership
across all async task boundaries:

src/rpc.rs (+718):
- Initialize top-level Cx via AgentCx::for_current_or_request() and
  install with asupersync::Cx::set_current at RPC entry point
- Clone and propagate parent Cx into all runtime_handle.spawn() call
  sites: extension commands, prompt-with-retry, bash execution, and
  streaming prompts — eliminating 8 separate for_request() allocations
- Add cx.checkpoint() guard in retry loops to detect cancellation
  between retries instead of sleeping through cancelled requests
- Replace all session.lock(&cx) calls to use the propagated bash_cx/
  prompt_cx instead of creating fresh contexts per lock acquisition

src/session.rs (+284/-192):
- Extract save_jsonl_full_rewrite_blocking() and
  append_jsonl_entries_blocking() helper functions from inline closures
- Replace thread::spawn + oneshot channel pattern with
  asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking for session persistence, enabling
  proper Cx inheritance and cancellation support
- Remove finish_jsonl_worker_result() helper (no longer needed)
- Add #[cfg(test)] emit_set_name_deadline_probe for deadline observability
  in test builds

src/agent.rs (+118):
- Inherit parent Cx in agent execution paths instead of creating fresh
  contexts, ensuring budget and deadline flow through agent tool calls

src/interactive.rs (+29) / src/interactive/agent.rs (+201):
- Propagate Cx through interactive agent loops and command dispatch
- Ensure all spawned interactive tasks inherit the session Cx

src/main.rs (+4):
- Thread Cx through top-level initialization paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
